nodes.label: Epic 1: First-Run Experience & Onboarding (34 pts) — 5-step wizard, empty states, one-click Sheets bootstrap, sample data seeder, confirmation screen, help tooltips
Epic 2: User & Team Management (29 pts) — team management page, invite flow, user profile, RBAC UI visibility, session management, persist actor_role
Epic 3: Approval UX Hardening (26 pts) — evidence panel, confidence breakdown, list improvements, expiry handling, Slack deep link, history tab, mobile-responsive
Epic 4: Operations Dashboard Enhancements (21 pts) — runway tile chart, AP aging, AR summary, compliance calendar view, CSV export, bank transactions search
Epic 5: Document Management (18 pts) — documents page registry, in-browser PDF preview, document re-generation, employee document portal
Epic 6: In-App Notification System (13 pts) — notification bell badge, history page, preferences per user, browser push (PWA)
Epic 7: Error Handling & Reliability (21 pts) — health dashboard UI, agent run retry, Sheets quota handling, LLM timeout fallback, vendor disambiguation UI, stale workflow cleanup
Epic 8: Agent Quality & Domain Logic (21 pts) — payroll auto-TDS calculation, AP payment initiation, AR automated collections sequence T+0/T+7/T+14
Epic 9: Security & Access Baseline (13 pts) — CSP headers + rate limiting, input sanitisation/prompt injection prevention, audit log row hashing, secure file upload
Activation blocker: ~80% drop-off before first agent run due to no onboarding wizard, requires manual CLI Sheets setup and env var role assignment
Velocity reference: 2-engineer team ~20 pts/sprint, 2-week sprints, V1 ~10 sprints (~5 months). Priority: Epics 1→2→3→9→8→rest
V1 Current Role Model (4 roles, env-var assigned): founder, finance_lead, hr_lead, employee — no UI, no invite flow, developer must edit .env.local to change roles
V1 Target Role Model (6 roles, UI-assigned): founder, finance_lead, hr_lead, manager, accountant, employee — managed via user_roles tab in SHEETS_MASTER

V2 Enterprise Role Hierarchy: Super Admin → Entity Admin → Finance (CFO/Finance Manager/AP Specialist/AR Specialist/Read-Only Auditor) / HR (HR Head/HR Manager/Payroll Specialist/Recruiter) / Operations (COO/Dept Head) / Employee

Delegation of Authority (DoA) matrix: configurable per organization. Action thresholds in INR mapped to approver roles (AP Specialist auto up to 10k, Finance Manager up to 1L, CFO up to 10L, Founder above 10L)
V2 Role Permissions Model: 3-level (Module, Action, Scope). Scope can be own/department/cost_center/entity/all. Custom roles via configs/rbac/custom_roles.json with inherits+overrides
Multi-level Approval Chains (V2) configured per org in configs/policies/approval_chains.json — sequential steps with role, timeout_hours, on_timeout escalation
Approval Delegation: OOO time-bounded delegation via POST /api/approvals/delegate. PolicyEngine checks delegation records before routing. All delegations audit-logged
SSO Integration (V2): SAML 2.0 (Okta/Azure AD/Ping/ADFS) + OIDC (Google Workspace/Okta/Auth0). JIT provisioning. Group-to-role mapping via configs/auth/sso_mapping.json
Security Controls comparison V1 vs V2: V1=6 roles/Google OAuth/no MFA; V2=custom roles/SAML+OIDC/TOTP for finance+founder/IP allowlisting/API key management/SOC2 Type I target

Velo product vision: AI operating system for back-office functions. Three-layer proposition: Execution (agents act), Governance (PolicyEngine+confidence ensures right human approves), Intelligence (surface risk before humans look)
Target customers: V1=10-200 person Indian startup, founder/finance lead juggling back-office manually. V2+=Series B+ / enterprise needing multi-entity ops, audit compliance, ERP integration

Velo architecture: pnpm monorepo, 4 packages — core (PolicyEngine/ConfidenceScorer/AuditLogger), agents (ReAct runtime, 8 specialist + 5 sub-agents), tools (100+ sheet tools, Slack/Resend/OCR/PDF/Drive), web (Next.js 14 command center)

Agent Roster: Orchestrator, Runway, Compliance, Payroll (sub: document-generator), AP Invoice (sub: invoice-extractor, expense-classifier, vendor-matcher, duplicate-detector), AR Collections, HR (sub: document-generator), Helpdesk. Sub-agents also include tax-planning

Phase 3 maturity: Core Engine production-ready, Agents ~80-90% complete (payroll TDS partial, AP payment placeholder, AR follow-up absent), Web UI ~90% (onboarding/notifications/team management/reports/document viewer missing), 6 connectors
Not yet wired: live bank API, GST portal integration, accounting system sync (Tally/Zoho/QuickBooks), decision memory for historical pattern scoring, cron jobs on host scheduler

Orchestrator agent system prompt: primary interface, routes to specialist agents via internal.sub_agent.invoke, answers platform/health queries via internal.platform.healthcheck, India-first context, ₹25k auto-threshold
Orchestrator routing design decision: never show routing menu to user, call internal.sub_agent.invoke in same turn with sub_agent_id and full input string — delegate, don't duplicate

internal.platform.healthcheck tool: returns checks (integration probes) + operational_snapshot (live Sheets data: pending approvals, compliance, AP/AR counts, bank balance, HR blockers, attention_items) + full *_detail arrays

Orchestrator India-specific context: FY April-March, INR, GST GSTR-1+3B, TDS monthly+quarterly 24Q, PF 12%/3.67%/8.33% capped ₹15k basic, ESIC ≤₹21k gross, PT Maharashtra ₹200/300, payment auto-threshold ₹25,000

Payroll agent system prompt: monthly pipeline — fetch employees/structures/attendance, compute gross, apply PF/ESIC/PT/TDS deductions, compute net, create payroll run + slips + compliance obligations, always requires approval

Payroll computation rules: Basic=CTC×basic_pct, HRA=Basic×hra_pct, LTA=CTC×lta_pct, Special Allowance=residual. LOP=(Gross÷working_days)×lop_days. PF employee 12% basic capped ₹1800. ESIC 0.75% gross if ≤₹21k. PT state-specific. TDS monthly instalment of projected annual

AP Invoice agent system prompt: 9-step pipeline — extract, match vendor, classify expense, detect duplicates, create AP entry, update GST input ledger, create expense entry, upload to Drive, initiate payment workflow (auto if <₹25k and payee exists, else approval)
AP Invoice state machine: PENDING_EXTRACTION → EXTRACTED → CLASSIFIED → VENDOR_MATCHED → PENDING_APPROVAL/AUTO_SCHEDULED → APPROVED → PAYMENT_INITIATED → PAID

AP Invoice India GST context: ITC eligibility conditions, Section 17(5) blocked credits (motor vehicles/food/club/health insurance), partial ITC for mixed use, RCM for import of services/GTA/advocate fees, GSTR-2B reconciliation required

AR Collections agent system prompt: track outstanding AR by client and ageing bucket (Current/0-30/31-60/61-90/>90 days), execute follow-up sequences T+7/T+14/T+30/T+45, compute collection probability, generate AR invoices (approval required), record payments
AR ageing collection probabilities: Current=95%, 0-30 days=85%, 31-60 days=65%, 61-90 days=35%, >90 days=15% (bad debt risk flag)

AR Collections India GST: IGST for inter-state clients, CGST+SGST for intra-state. Most IT/SaaS=18%. E-invoicing mandatory for turnover >₹5Cr (IRN from IRP). Export of services zero-rated, requires LUT filing

Tax Planning sub-agent (served via Helpdesk): compare old vs new tax regime, identify unused deductions, compute projected tax under both regimes, advise on 80C/80D/HRA/NPS/Section 24(b), employee-facing only
New regime FY2025-26 slabs: 0% up to ₹3L, 5% ₹3L-₹7L, 10% ₹7L-₹10L, 15% ₹10L-₹12L, 20% ₹12L-₹15L, 30% above ₹15L. Standard deduction ₹75k. No 80C/HRA/LTA. NPS 80CCD(2) employer still allowed
Old regime slabs: 0% up to ₹2.5L, 5% ₹2.5L-₹5L, 20% ₹5L-₹10L, 30% above ₹10L. Standard deduction ₹50k. Allows HRA/LTA/80C(₹1.5L)/80D/80CCD(1B)(₹50k)/Section 24(b)(₹2L self-occupied)
Vendor Matcher sub-agent: GSTIN match (confidence 0.98 if found) → name fuzzy match (≥0.90 HIGH, 0.70-0.89 MEDIUM review, 0.50-0.69 LOW create_new, <0.50 no match). Reports is_payee_added status
Vendor matching strategy: Step 1 GSTIN exact (gold standard), Step 2 name fuzzy after normalizing legal suffixes and abbreviations, Step 3 new vendor decision if confidence <0.50
Expense Classifier sub-agent (GST-aware): classify invoice line items into category/sub-category, determine ITC eligibility per Section 17(5), validate GST rate, output confidence score and reasoning

Expense category taxonomy: Technology(SaaS/Cloud/Licenses), Professional Services, Office & Admin, Marketing & Sales, Travel & Logistics, HR & Recruitment, R&D, Employee Benefits (mostly blocked ITC), Capital Expenditure, Motor Vehicles (blocked ITC), Miscellaneous

Section 17(5) Blocked ITC: motor vehicles, food/beverages/catering, beauty/health services, club memberships, health/life insurance (unless statutory), employee gifts >₹50k/year, personal transportation, construction of immovable property

Duplicate Detector sub-agent: primary keys exact match (vendor_id+invoice_number+date) = 0.99 confidence. Secondary fuzzy match (same vendor+number different date, or same vendor+date+amount within 1%) = 0.85 confidence. Recurring subscription pattern = 0.10 (not duplicate)
Duplicate detector recommended actions: proceed, block_and_notify, flag_for_review. Always report existing invoice status (PAID duplicate more urgent than PENDING)
Compliance agent system prompt: maintain statutory calendar, send 7-day and 2-day alerts, prefill filing data from ledger, mark filings done. Never auto-file — all filings require explicit approval

Statutory filing calendar: GST (GSTR-1 11th/13th, GSTR-3B 20th/22nd/24th, GSTR-9 31st Dec), TDS (deposit 7th, 24Q/26Q quarterly, Form 16 15th June), PF (EPF challan+ECR 15th), ESIC challan 15th, PT Maharashtra last day of month
Compliance penalty context: GST late fee ₹50/day (₹20 nil returns), TDS interest 1.5%/month, PF late interest 12%/year, ESIC damage up to 25%

Employee Helpdesk agent: self-service for own payslip, leave balances/history, HR policy lookup, tax planning via TaxPlanning sub-agent. Critical: only access actor_id's own data, never expose another employee's data

Leave types: CL 12/year (no carry-forward, max 3 consecutive), SL 12/year (medical cert >2 days), EL accrues 1.25 days/month (carry-forward up to 30 days, encashable at F&F), Maternity 26 weeks, LOP when balance zero

Document Generator sub-agent: generates employment and invoice documents (offer letters, salary slips, experience certificates). Uses documents.* tools. Never invents salary numbers — halts and requests missing fields

HR agent system prompt: onboarding (employee record + salary structure + leave balances + offer letter + tasks + welcome email), offboarding (exit checklist + F&F + relieving letter), leave management, policy management, document generation, hiring support

HR onboarding checklist: create employee record (PAN/Aadhaar/bank mandatory), assign salary structure, initialize CL:12/SL:12/EL:0, generate+send offer letter, create onboarding tasks (IT/bank verification/PF/ESIC registration), send welcome notification

HR India-specific compliance: Shops & Establishment Act (state registration), Maternity Benefit Act 1961 (26 weeks, 50+ employees creche), POSH Act (ICC mandatory 10+ employees), Gratuity (5 years, 15 days × years), F&F within 30-45 days

Runway agent system prompt: compute cash runway, burn rate breakdown (payroll/vendors/compliance/other), factor committed payables and expected AR collections with probability weighting, hiring impact simulations, alert at amber (4mo)/red (2mo)/critical (1mo) thresholds

Runway computation: Monthly Burn = trailing 3-month average (Payroll+AP+Compliance+Other). Available Cash = Bank Balance − Committed Unpaid Payables. AR Adjustment = Σ(amount × collection_probability). Runway = (Available Cash + Expected Collections) ÷ Monthly Burn

Invoice Extractor sub-agent: extracts 18+ structured fields from raw invoice text (OCR or plain text) including GSTIN validation, GST component separation (IGST vs CGST+SGST), total check, RCM detection. confidence = 0.95 if all mandatory fields present with total validation

Invoice extractor mandatory fields: invoice_number, invoice_date (normalize to YYYY-MM-DD), vendor_name, vendor_gstin (15-char format validation), buyer_name, buyer_gstin, subtotal, gst_amount, total_amount. Missing any = extraction_complete: false, confidence −0.20 each

@velo/web: Next.js Command Center — React UI, Route Handlers, Prisma. No separate backend — APIs are Next.js routes under app/api/. Agent logic in @velo/agents, shared policy/config in @velo/core, Sheets/Drive/email tools in @velo/tools

Sample contract/SOW domain concepts: Master Services Agreement, Exhibit A (scope), SLA (response time in business hours), remedy cap (fees paid in prior 3 months), confidentiality survival period (2-6 years), milestone dependencies, governing law India

Sample multi-invoice domain: AR invoice fragments with invoice number (NVF-APR26-NNN format), bill-to client (Horizon Retail/Vertex Mobility/Sterling Foods), amount plus GST, HSN 9983, missing place of supply (stress test for extractor)

Sample vendor invoice: Novaforge Labs Pvt Ltd buyer (GSTIN 27AABCNova1Z5), CloudScale Infra Solutions LLP vendor (GSTIN 29AABCUCS01Z2), multi-line cloud hosting tiers at 18% GST, invoice CSI-APR-2042. Technology/Cloud Infrastructure category, ITC claimable
Bank narration domain concepts: transaction types (NEFT/IMPS/UPI/IFT/CARD), DR/CR direction, reference numbers, narration categories (vendor payout batch, client receipt, statutory adjustment)
Helpdesk knowledge domain: LTA/medical/WFH stipend claims (submit bills in portal, finance SLA 5 days), policy document references (P-N format), employee self-service Q&A pattern

OCR noise stress test: synthetic OCR character confusion (l/1/I/0/O), mixed INR/EUR currency symbols, line item with qty/rate/tax/ref fields, NEFT reference numbers — tests invoice extractor robustness

Design decision: all high-stakes actions (payroll disbursement, GST filing, AR invoice sending, offer letters, AP payment >₹25k, termination, follow-up emails) require explicit human approval — agents never auto-execute these

Design decision: Google Sheets as primary data store (5 workbooks: CONFIG/MASTER/TRANSACTIONS/COMPLIANCE/LOGS). Trade-off: accessible/low-infra for SME target but 60 req/min quota limit is a production risk requiring queue/retry handling
Design decision: all rules, rates, and lists in /configs JSON or Sheets — never hardcoded. Enables per-org customization (DoA matrix, approval chains, RBAC, custom roles) without code changes
Role: finance_lead — AP/AR/compliance/bank/payroll view/approval.resolve. V2: also Finance Manager (up to ₹1L DoA), CFO (up to ₹10L DoA)
Role: founder — all actions including terminate_employee, file_gst_return, run_payroll. Ultimate approver. DoA: all amounts above ₹10L
Role: employee — own payslip, own leave, own documents, helpdesk queries only
GST ITC eligibility concept: claimable if vendor is GST-registered (valid GSTIN), invoice in buyer's name, goods/services for business use, not in Section 17(5) blocked list, appears in GSTR-2B
Reverse Charge Mechanism (RCM): company pays GST directly to government for import of services, GTA, advocate fees — not to vendor. Flagged separately in AP Invoice processing

TDS on salary: monthly instalment of (projected annual taxable income − deductions) × slab rate ÷ 12. Recalculated monthly for investment declarations. Deposited by 7th of following month (30th for March). Quarterly returns 24Q
PF contribution: Employee 12% of basic (capped ₹1800/month at ₹15k basic). Employer EPF 3.67% + EPS 8.33% (capped ₹1250/month). EPF challan + ECR due 15th of following month
ESIC: applicable only for gross salary ≤₹21,000/month. Employee 0.75% of gross, Employer 3.25% of gross. Challan due 15th of following month
